com.mentor.chs.api

Class IXGraphicConversions.XSVGContext

  • java.lang.Object
    • com.mentor.chs.api.IXGraphicConversions.XBaseContext
      • com.mentor.chs.api.IXGraphicConversions.XSVGContext
  • Enclosing interface:
    IXGraphicConversions

    public static class IXGraphicConversions.XSVGContext
extends IXGraphicConversions.XBaseContext
    Class which need to be used as input while requesting for SVG of a diagram. Default values are filled and user can fill the values for which he need a change

    • Field Detail

      • m_generateLegacySVG

        public IXGraphicConversions.GenerateLegacySVG m_generateLegacySVG
        Where no dimension constraint (dpi, maxwidth, maxheight) is specified, the (pixel) dimensions of the output SVG are such that applying a 200 DPI conversion will result in the original Capital diagram physical dimensions. For instance, if the original Capital diagram has a border which (physical) width is 16.4 inch, then the output SVG (pixel) width will be 200 * 16.4 = 3280 SVG may be generated using Batik or CSVG (Mentor's own Technology for generating SVG). New applications should always choose CSVG as the files are substantially smaller (typically less than one quarter of the size) and render much faster, especially in mobile devices. However, for backward compatibility with historical versions and previously existing third-party software one may want to generate using Batik.

        By default the system will always choose to generate CSVG. See m_generate_Batik = false;

        The properties 'm_imageWidth,' 'm_imageHeight' 'm_dpi' 'm_strokeStretch' are only relevant to Batik Generation and may be safely ignored for CSVG.

        In CSVG generation all relevant properties are read from \chs_home\config\svgprops.xml. These include properties which set the initial size of the displayed SVG when the SVG file is directly opened in a web browser. However, software which displays SVG via HTML and JavaScript will take no notice of those dimensions.

        [All the follwing applies to Batik Generation only, CSVG always draws a correctly scaled diagram]

        Where no dimension constraint (dpi, maxwidth, maxheight) is specified, the (pixel) dimensions of the output SVG are such that applying a 200 DPI conversion will result in the original Capital diagram physical dimensions. For instance, if the original Capital diagram has a border which (physical) width is 16.4 inch, then the output SVG (pixel) width will be 200 * 16.4 = 3280

        The dpi parameter overrides the default 200 DPI constant. For instance, with the previous example, passing dpi=100 will result in an SVG width of 100 * 16.4 = 1640

        The maxwidth and maxheight parameters can be used instead of or in addition to the dpi parameter. They prevent the dimension to exceed a given (pixel) number, after the dpi scaling has been applied. If the dimension is exceeded, the coordinates are rescaled to fit, also preserving the aspect ratio. With the previous example, assuming the physical border height is 11.6 inch (A3), passing dpi=100 and maxwidth=1000 will result in the following SVG dimensions: width = 1000 height = (100 * 11.6) * (1000 / 1640) = 707 where the width/height aspect ratio is preserved: (1000 / 707) = 1.41 = (16.4 / 11.6)

        The contextualInfoToBeWritten parameter indicates whether draw elements pertaining to a particular object should be grouped in SVG (using the <g> element, and a �desc� element added to the group to describe which Capital object the draw elements refers).

      • m_imageWidth

        public int m_imageWidth

      • m_imageHeight

        public int m_imageHeight

      • m_dpi

        public double m_dpi

      • m_outputContextualInformation

        public boolean m_outputContextualInformation

      • m_strokeStretch

        public double m_strokeStretch

      • m_CSVGParameters

        public CSVGParameters m_CSVGParameters
        This member variable allows the user to programtically specify some parameters to the non-Legacy (CSVG) mechanism without the need to modify chs_home\config\svgprops.xml. If this member variable is set to null, then the values in svgprops.xml will be used.

      • color

        public IXGraphicConversions.XPDFColorChoice color
        Print color choice as per the XPDFColorChoice enumeration. Default: COLOR

    • Constructor Detail

      • XSVGContext

        public XSVGContext()
